prompt: Create an ultra-detailed sketchbook-style character exploration page on a pure bright white background, using my uploaded photo as a STRICT facial reference with absolute identity preservation. The character must unmistakably retain my exact facial structure, with zero facial drift, zero beautification, and no reinterpretation of identity. The page should look like an illustrator’s personal sketchbook filled organically over time, not like a clean or organized character sheet. Arrange the drawings in a spontaneous, overlapping, scattered composition with varying sizes and angles, as though an artist casually filled the page with ideas, expressions, experiments, notes, and visual jokes. Include a dynamic mix of: expressive full-body sketches, face close-ups, rough construction sketches, loose gesture drawings, tiny doodles, exaggerated reaction faces, chibi/deformed mini versions, casual pose studies, humorous scribbles, motion sketches, emotional expression tests, profile and three-quarter face angles, playful character exaggerations, unfinished line-art fragments, layered redraws and crossed-out sketch attempts. The overall art style should feel raw, energetic, intelligent, and highly expressive, with confident pencil strokes, messy line weight variation, rough ink marks, light shading, accidental smudges, handwritten notes, tiny arrows, annotations, and sketchbook imperfections. Preserve a handmade traditional animation-development feel. Visually interpret the character as: highly creative and analytical, tech-oriented and engineering-minded, observant and skeptical, quietly confident, humorous in a dry, understated way, stylish without trying too hard, mentally sharp but slightly chaotic from overthinking ideas, someone constantly experimenting, refining, and building things. The clothing designs should blend modern casual fashion with subtle creative-tech aesthetics: relaxed streetwear, football-inspired outfits, layered jackets or hoodies, clean sneakers, bracelets or small accessories, occasional Arsenal-inspired visual elements, casual poses that feel natural and self-assured. Some sketches should look polished while others remain rough and incomplete, creating the feeling of an authentic working sketchbook page. Include a few exaggerated comedic moments and expressive chibi versions that amplify personality while still maintaining recognizable identity cues from my face. Use mostly monochrome pencil and ink sketch aesthetics with selective soft accent colors if needed. Maintain strong visual rhythm and controlled chaos across the page. The final result should feel like a beloved main character concept page from an animated film, manga production notebook, or elite character designer’s private sketch collection.
